name: RDKit (Cheminformatics and Drug Discovery Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3); Greg Landrum / RDKit community

performance.notes: C++ core; fingerprint computation very fast; 3D conformer generation slower

agent_readiness.notes: De facto Python cheminformatics library. from rdkit import Chem. from rdkit.Chem import Draw, Descriptors, AllChem. mol = Chem.MolFromSmiles('CCO'). Draw.MolToImage(mol). fp = AllChem.GetMorganFingerprintAsBitVect(mol, radius=2, nBits=2048). Descriptors.MolWt(mol). Chem.RWMol for editing. SMARTS substructure search. 3D conformers: AllChem.EmbedMolecule(mol). Energy minimization: AllChem.MMFFOptimizeMolecule(mol). Similarity: DataStructs.TanimotoSimilarity. PandasTools for DataFrame integration. Reaction SMARTS. Virtual screening. Drug-likeness: Lipinski filters.

gotchas: Non-Pythonic API patterns in some modules (legacy C++ style)
Documentation can be sparse for advanced features — tutorials recommended
Memory management with explicit mol objects requires care
best_when: Drug discovery: ADMET property prediction, lead optimization
ML feature generation for molecular property prediction
avoid_when: Simple molecular formula parsing (pubchempy or chempy simpler)
summary: RDKit is the de facto Python cheminformatics library. Chem.MolFromSmiles('CCO') parses SMILES. Morgan fingerprints (ECFP) for ML features. Molecular descriptors: MolWt, LogP, TPSA. 3D conformer generation with MMFF/UFF minimization. Tanimoto similarity for virtual screening. Reaction SMARTS for chemical transformations. PandasTools for DataFrame workflows. Drug-likeness filters (Lipinski). C++ core via conda. Essential for drug discovery and computational chemistry.
